Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot subvendor onboarding — can we proceed without PO?

Hi Connor,

Quick ask — we're running a small integration pilot with a HelixOps subcontractor (Lumen Labs) who'll be doing two weeks of instrumentation work for the integration. The engagement will be split across two invoices (~$1,800 each). Lumen is set up as a subcontractor under our agreement and cannot wait for a formal PO (their procurement cycle is much slower).

Is Redwood able to accept invoice-only onboarding for this pilot (no PO) so we can start next week? We can provide the W-9, insurance certificate, statement of work, and a signed subcontract. If needed, we can restrict it to a single invoice cap.

Appreciate a quick note on the path forward — timeline is tight.

Thank...

Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing a spike in 5xx responses and timeouts from Redwood's generate endpoint around 06:30 PST this m...
text:
From: Amy Liu <amy.liu@hightowerretail.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jan 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: High error rate / timeouts from our retail checkout (us-west)

Hi Rafael,

We started seeing a spike in 5xx responses and timeouts from Redwood's generate endpoint around 06:30 PST this morning. Impacting live checkout inference: pages 10–20% error rate and lots of slow responses (~3–6s increased latency). We haven't changed our client code.

Sample request IDs and timestamps attached in the JSON (customer-logs-sample.json). The errors are mainly 524/connection-timeout and occasional 502s. The traffic is routed to the us-west model pool.

Can you confirm if anything is happening on your side (peering, NAT exhaustion, proxy resets, or DNS changes)? This is causing checkout slowdowns for a subset of sessions. We're on a renewal discussion so this is urgent.

...

Cc: security@acme-corp.com
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: SIG follow-up: encryption specifics (in transit / at rest / KMS)

Hi Rafael,

Thanks for sending the completed SIG spreadsheet earlier. Our infosec team reviewed the answers and flagged a few follow-ups specifically around encryption and key management before we can close the intake ticket:

- Clarify exactly which TLS versions and cipher suites you accept for platform API endpoints (we need to ensure TLS 1.2+ and AEAD ciphers).
- For data at rest, is envelope encryption used? Who manages DEKs and KEKs?
- Do you offer HSM-backed keys or BYOK for enterprise/private deployments? Any details on how you rotate keys (frequency, automation, and rewrap strategy)?
- Evidence request: architecture diagram showing KMS placement, and ...

Hi team,

We're scheduling a targeted maintenance activity in eu-west to deploy a critical NIC driver security patch that addresses an out-of-band CVE affecting some 100Gbps NIC microcode paths. The change requires a rolling tenant-aware drain for affected hosts to preserve KV cache and avoid elevated tail latency during the update.

Why this matters: the patch fixes a vulnerability that could cause packet drops under specific workloads and we've observed small error-rate spikes on a canary fleet. We want to be proactive for customers with high-th...

Thanks for sending over the RFI. Attached is our initial response for Section A (controls, uptime targets, and audit artifacts). Highlights:
- We meet FFIEC-aligned resilience expectations; runbooks and failover tests conducted quarterly.
- SOC 2 Type II available; summary attached.
- Network diagram and isolation controls attached.

Two clarifications we expect you may ask for:
1) If you want raw logs or a scoped environment for audit review we can provide a limited read-only access window.
2) For on-prem / private deployments we can supply an architecture pack outl...
